title: First Solar Q4 Earnings and 2026 Guidance Analysis
text: First Solar reported mixed quarterly performance with $1.68 billion in revenue, exceeding analyst estimates, but delivered an EPS of $4.84, which fell short of the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $5.22. Despite the earnings miss, full-year 2025 showed strength with record module sales of 17.5 GW and $5.2 billion in annual revenue. The primary catalyst for the stock decline was management's cautious forward guidance. The company projects 2026 net sales between $4.9-$5.2 billion, representing essentially flat performance versus 2025. This modest growth trajectory disappointed investors expecting stronger expansion in the renewable sector. Management cited volatile tax and accounting effects and uncertainties around global taxation frameworks as contributors to the conservative stance. The company notably refrained from providing explicit EPS guidance. Despite near-term headwinds, First Solar maintains positioning advantages through proprietary thin-film cadmium telluride CdTe technology differentiation, expanded U.S. manufacturing capacity enabling $2.10-$2.19 billion in Section 45X tax credits for 2026, and plans to reach 25 GW global capacity by end-2026, with 14 GW domestic. The broader solar sector faces the first year of negative growth in global PV demand in over a decade, with forecasted demand declining to 529-624 GWdc in 2026 from 653-706 GWdc in 2025.
